title: Ezra and the Magic Acorn
synopsis: When young Ezra finds a magical golden acorn in the forest, he discovers it belongs to Nutkin the squirrel's grandmother. Together with his diverse group of animal friends, Ezra must return the acorn to help Grandma Hazel remember her favorite stories before the autumn moon sets.

pages.text: Ezra loved exploring the Whispering Woods. His dark curly hair bounced as he walked down the forest path. The leaves were turning orange and red. It was his favorite time of year. "I wonder what I'll find today," he said to himself. Ezra always found something special in the woods. Sometimes it was a pretty rock. Sometimes it was a funny bug. But today would be different. Today would be magical.

illustration.description: A cheerful Black boy with dark curly hair wearing a green jacket and brown boots walking through an autumn forest. Colorful leaves are falling around him, with orange, red, and yellow trees creating a magical woodland setting. His expression is curious and excited, with big bright eyes looking ahead.
illustration.alt: Ezra walking through autumn forest with falling leaves

pages.text: As Ezra walked deeper into the woods, something sparkled under a big oak tree. He bent down to look closer. There, half-buried under some leaves, was a golden acorn. It glowed like a tiny sun. "Wow!" Ezra whispered. He picked up the acorn carefully. It felt warm in his hands. The acorn began to glow even brighter. Suddenly, he heard a small voice crying nearby.

illustration.description: Ezra kneeling by a large oak tree, holding a glowing golden acorn in his cupped hands. The acorn radiates warm golden light, illuminating his amazed face. Autumn leaves scatter around the tree base, and magical sparkles dance in the air around the acorn.
illustration.alt: Ezra discovering the magical glowing golden acorn

pages.text: "Someone help me! Please!" came the tiny voice. Ezra looked around and saw a small gray squirrel sitting on a tree branch. The squirrel had tears in his big brown eyes. "What's wrong?" Ezra asked kindly. "My name is Nutkin," said the squirrel. "My grandmother is very sick. She can't remember her favorite stories anymore. The magic acorn you found belongs to her. It holds all her memories!"

illustration.description: A small gray squirrel with big teary brown eyes sitting on a tree branch, looking down at Ezra who is standing below holding the golden acorn. The squirrel appears sad and worried, while Ezra looks up with a compassionate expression. Dappled forest light filters through the leaves.
illustration.alt: Nutkin the squirrel crying on a branch while talking to Ezra

pages.text: "I want to help your grandmother," Ezra said. "But how?" Nutkin wiped his eyes with a tiny paw. "We must take the acorn to Grandma Hazel before the autumn moon sets tonight. If we don't, she'll forget her stories forever!" Just then, Luna the owl swooped down. She had beautiful speckled feathers and wise golden eyes. "I heard everything," Luna said. "I know the way to Grandma Hazel's tree house. But it's very far."

illustration.description: A wise-looking owl with speckled brown and white feathers and large golden eyes perched near Ezra and Nutkin. Luna has an intelligent, kind expression. The forest setting shows late afternoon light filtering through trees, creating a warm but urgent atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Luna the owl joining Ezra and Nutkin in the forest

pages.text: "We can help too!" called out a cheerful voice. Rosita the rabbit hopped over with her friend Kenji the fox. Rosita had soft brown fur and wore a red ribbon. Kenji had orange fur with black-tipped ears and a friendly smile. "We know shortcuts through the forest," said Kenji. "And I'm very fast," added Rosita. "We're all friends here. Friends help each other!" Ezra smiled. He loved having so many helpful friends.

illustration.description: A brown rabbit wearing a red ribbon and an orange fox with black-tipped ears standing together near Ezra. Both animals have big expressive eyes and friendly smiles. The group is gathered in a circle, showing teamwork and friendship in the warm forest setting.
illustration.alt: Rosita the rabbit and Kenji the fox joining the group of friends

pages.text: The friends began their journey through the Whispering Woods. Ezra held the golden acorn carefully. It grew dimmer as the sun started to set. "We must hurry," said Luna, flying above them. Rosita and Kenji led the way down a winding path. Nutkin rode on Ezra's shoulder. "Tell me about your grandmother," Ezra said. "She's the best storyteller in the whole forest," Nutkin replied. "She knows stories about brave knights, silly dragons, and magical adventures. I don't want her to forget them!"

illustration.description: The group of friends traveling together on a forest path at sunset. Ezra walks in the center with Nutkin on his shoulder, while Rosita and Kenji lead the way and Luna flies overhead. The golden acorn's light is getting dimmer, and the sky shows orange and pink sunset colors through the trees.
illustration.alt: The friends traveling together through the forest at sunset

pages.text: As the moon began to rise, the friends reached a very tall tree. High up in the branches was a cozy tree house with warm light glowing in the windows. "That's Grandma Hazel's home," said Nutkin sadly. They climbed up the tree using a spiral staircase carved into the trunk. Inside the tree house, they found an elderly squirrel with silver fur sitting in a rocking chair. She looked confused and sad. "Grandma, I brought help," Nutkin said softly.

illustration.description: A tall tree with a charming tree house built into its branches, with warm yellow light glowing from round windows. A spiral staircase winds around the trunk. The moon is visible through the leaves, and the friends are climbing up toward the cozy home. The tree house has a whimsical, fairy-tale appearance.
illustration.alt: Grandma Hazel's tree house high up in a tall tree with moonlight

pages.text: Grandma Hazel looked at her visitors with cloudy eyes. "I'm sorry, little ones," she said in a gentle voice. "I used to tell wonderful stories, but I can't remember them anymore. My mind feels like an empty book." Ezra stepped forward with the golden acorn. "We found something that belongs to you," he said kindly. As soon as Grandma Hazel touched the acorn, it began to glow brightly again. Her eyes sparkled with joy!

illustration.description: Inside the cozy tree house, an elderly squirrel with silver fur and kind but confused eyes sits in a rocking chair. Ezra is offering her the glowing golden acorn, which radiates bright warm light. The tree house interior has bookshelves, soft cushions, and a homey atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Ezra giving the golden acorn to Grandma Hazel inside the tree house

pages.text: "My stories!" Grandma Hazel exclaimed. "They're all coming back to me!" She hugged the golden acorn close to her heart. "Thank you, dear boy, and all of you wonderful friends." She looked at each of them with twinkling eyes. "Now, who would like to hear the tale of the Dancing Dragon who loved to bake cookies?" All the friends cheered and gathered around her rocking chair. Ezra smiled as Grandma Hazel began to tell the silliest, most wonderful story he had ever heard. The magic acorn glowed warmly in her paws, keeping all her precious memories safe forever.

illustration.description: All the friends gathered around Grandma Hazel's rocking chair in the warm, cozy tree house. She's holding the glowing acorn and telling an animated story, with everyone listening with big smiles and wide, excited eyes. The scene shows friendship, joy, and the magic of storytelling with warm golden light filling the room.
illustration.alt: Everyone gathered around Grandma Hazel as she tells stories with the magical acorn

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What did Ezra find under the oak tree?
questions.explanation: Ezra found a golden acorn that glowed like a tiny sun under the big oak tree.

questions.question: Who did the golden acorn belong to?
questions.explanation: The golden acorn belonged to Grandma Hazel, Nutkin's grandmother, and it held all her story memories.

questions.question: What color ribbon did Rosita the rabbit wear?
questions.explanation: Rosita the rabbit wore a red ribbon and was one of Ezra's helpful friends.

questions.question: Where did Grandma Hazel live?
questions.explanation: Grandma Hazel lived in a cozy tree house high up in the branches of a very tall tree.

questions.question: What happened when Grandma Hazel touched the acorn?
questions.explanation: When Grandma Hazel touched the golden acorn, it glowed brightly and she remembered all her wonderful stories.

pages.title: Complete the Story
pages.text: Help finish this sentence from the story! Ezra found a _____ acorn under the big oak _____. The acorn belonged to Grandma _____ and held all her story _____.

pages.title: Who Said What?
pages.text: Match the character to what they said! _____ said 'My stories! They're all coming back to me!' _____ said 'We know shortcuts through the forest.' _____ said 'I heard everything. I know the way.'
